name: set_fold_empty_returns_zero
reason: If folding an empty set didn't return the zero element, the base case would inject phantom values

name: set_fold_infinite_returns_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_fold_infinite_returns_zero(s: Set<int>, zero: int, f: spec_fn(int, int) -> int)
    requires
        !s.finite(),
    ensures
        set_fold(s, zero, f) == zero,
{
}
reason: Folding an infinite set silently returns `zero` instead of being undefined — callers may not realize they get the identity element for non-finite sets

name: map_fold_empty_is_empty
reason: Mapping over an empty set should produce empty — if it didn't, map_fold would fabricate elements from nothing

name: set_fold_singleton_applies_f
reason: Folding a singleton should apply f exactly once to (zero, element) — any other result means set_fold mishandles the base-to-single-element transition

name: set_fold_order_dependent_non_commutative
reason: set_fold uses `s.choose()` which is deterministic but unspecified — for non-commutative f the result is well-defined but unpredictable, yet the spec provides no commutativity requirement
